text:
#### Confirmation of a provisional order made in a reciprocating country E+W


34.16. —(1) This rule applies to proceedings for the confirmation of a provisional order made in a reciprocating country [ F35 , including proceedings in the family court for the confirmation of a provisional order made in a reciprocating country varying a maintenance order to which section 5(5) or 9(6) of the 1972 Act applies ] .


(2) Paragraph (3) applies on receipt by the court of—


(3) On receipt of the documents referred to in paragraph (2)—


(4) The date fixed for the hearing must be not less than 21 days beginning with the date on which the court officer sent the documents to the payer in accordance with paragraph (2).


(5) The court officer will send to the relevant court in the reciprocating country a certified copy of any order confirming or refusing to confirm the provisional order.


F36 (6) . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .


(Section 5(5) and 7 of the 1972 Act provide for proceedings for the confirmation of a provisional order.) F37 ...


Textual Amendments


F35 Words in rule 34.16(1) inserted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No.3) Rules 2013 (S.I. 2013/3204) , rules 1 , 113(a) (with rule 137 ); S.I. 2014/954 , art. 2


F36 Rule 34.16(6) omitted (22.4.2014) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No.3) Rules 2013 (S.I. 2013/3204) , rules 1 , 113(b) (with rule 137 ); S.I. 2014/954 , art. 2


F37 Words in rule 34.16 omitted (22.4.2014) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No.3) Rules 2013 (S.I. 2013/3204) , rules 1 , 113(c) (with rule 137 ); S.I. 2014/954 , art. 2

metadata.title: Part 34 — Reciprocal Enforcement of Maintenance Orders
